question: What is the structure of a `charmcraft.yaml` file for a Kubernetes charm? Show a complete example with all required fields.

claude_output.result:
Here's the complete structure of a `charmcraft.yaml` for a Kubernetes charm:

## Required fields

```yaml
name: my-app-k8s          # lowercase, hyphens only
type: charm
```

## Recommended fields

```yaml
title: My App on Kubernetes
summary: Short one-line description (under 100 chars)

description: |
  Full markdown description of the charm.
  Appears on the Charmhub page.
```

## Kubernetes-specific requirements

```yaml
assumes:
  - juju >= 3.1
  - k8s-api             # Declares this needs a K8s substrate

containers:
  my-app:
    resource: my-image  # Must match a resource name below
    mounts:
      - storage: data   # Optional: mount a storage volume
        location: /data

resources:
  my-image:
    type: oci-image
    description: "Application container image"
```

## Build configuration (required)

```yaml
bases:
  - build-on:
      - name: ubuntu
        channel: "22.04"
    run-on:
      - name: ubuntu
        channel: "22.04"

parts:
  charm:
    plugin: uv
    source: .
    build-snaps:
      - astral-uv
```

## Complete example

```yaml
name: my-app-k8s
type: charm
title: My App on Kubernetes
summary: Deploys and operates My App on Kubernetes.

description: |
  My App is a web application. This charm deploys and operates
  it on Kubernetes using the Charmed Operator Framework.

assumes:
  - juju >= 3.1
  - k8s-api

bases:
  - build-on:
      - name: ubuntu
        channel: "22.04"
    run-on:
      - name: ubuntu
        channel: "22.04"

parts:
  charm:
    plugin: uv
    source: .
    build-snaps:
      - astral-uv

containers:
  my-app:
    resource: my-app-image
    mounts:
      - storage: data
        location: /var/lib/myapp

resources:
  my-app-image:
    type: oci-image
    description: "My App container image"

config:
  options:
    port:
      type: int
      description: "Port the application listens on"
      default: 8080
    log-level:
      type: string
      description: "Logging level (debug/info/warning/error)"
      default: "info"

provides:
  website:
    interface: http

requires:
  database:
    interface: postgresql_client
    optional: true
  ingress:
    interface: ingress
    optional: true
    limit: 1
  certificates:
    interface: tls-certificates
    optional: true

peers:
  app-peers:
    interface: my-app-peers

storage:
  data:
    type: filesystem
    location: /var/lib/myapp
    minimum-size: 1G

actions:
  rotate-secret:
    description: "Rotate the application secret key"
    additionalProperties: false
  get-admin-password:
    description: "Retrieve the admin password"
    additionalProperties: false

charm-libs:
  - lib: data_platform_libs.data_interfaces
    version: "0"

links:
  documentation: https://discourse.charmhub.io/t/my-app-k8s-docs/12345
  issues: https://github.com/canonical/my-app-k8s/issues
  source: https://github.com/canonical/my-app-k8s
```

## Key differences from a machine charm

| | Kubernetes | Machine |
|---|---|---|
| `assumes` | Needs `k8s-api` | Not needed |
| `containers` | Required | Not used |
| `resources` | `oci-image` type | `file` type (optional) |
| Workload interaction | Via Pebble | Via `apt`/`snap`/`subprocess` |

## Important rules to follow

- **Always** include `optional: true` or `optional: false` on every `requires` relation — never rely on the default
- **Always** include `additionalProperties: false` in action definitions
- The `containers` entry must reference a name defined under `resources` with `type: oci-image`
- `charm-libs` is only for libraries not on PyPI; prefer PyPI packages where available
